methods.description: Collection and storage of bee pollen samples from various regions in Argentina for subsequent bacterial isolation.

steps.description: A total of 30 bee pollen samples were freshly obtained from beekeepers from different producing areas of Argentina.
steps.searchable_summary: collect bee pollen samples from various regions in Argentina

steps.description: All the samples were transported to the laboratory and stored at 4 C until testing within seven days of receiving.
steps.searchable_summary: store pollen samples at 4 C for up to seven days

methods.description: Procedure for isolating spore-forming bacteria from bee pollen samples using a series of chemical and thermal treatments followed by plating on selective media.

steps.description: 1 g of pollen grains per sample was mixed with 9 ml of 0.01 M sodium phosphate buffer saline (pH 7.2), agitated for 40 min, and filtered through Whatman # 3 to a sterile 15-ml Falcon TM centrifuge tube.
steps.searchable_summary: mix pollen with buffer, agitate, and filter
inputs: 9 ml 0.01 M sodium phosphate buffer saline

steps.description: Tubes were centrifuged at 9500 g for 30 min at 4 C, and the supernatant was discarded to leave 3 ml of fluid.
Tubes were vortex-mixed for 2 min and thermally treated at 80 C for 10 min.
steps.searchable_summary: vortex mix and heat treat pollen solution

steps.description: Aliquots of 100 ml each were plated by duplicate over the surface of MYPGP agar supplemented with 6 mg/ml nalidixic acid and 10 mg/ml pipemidic acid for the isolation of Paenibacillus larvae, and HiChrome Bacillus agar for isolation of the rest of spore-forming bacteria.
Plates were incubated at 37 C under microaerophilic conditions for MYPGP, and under aerobic conditions for Hichrome.

Plates were examined daily until bacterial growth developed and colony counts were made. The number of colonies was averaged, and the total colony-forming units (CFUs) were calculated per g of pollen (CFU/g).
steps.searchable_summary: examine plates, count colonies, calculate CFUs
